topic: NMP drift 2.26x longitudinal — post-EXP-297 verification (defender-pivot + W019 + axial-symmetry + PRED-R004 forward falsification)
change_summary: EXP-297 integrated 2026-05-10T01:19:06Z. P1 appended Defender-pivot anticipated paragraph to detail_verdict_text (1136→3152 chars). P2 set detail_extra from null to 1574-char block carrying W019 self-contradiction + Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-ref + PRED-R004 forward-falsification flag. ISS-1918 / ISS-1902 / ISS-1903 closed.
our_argument_summary: WIN-043 claims a 2.26× longitudinal:latitudinal NMP drift ratio. Our detail_evidence shows it is post-hoc arithmetic from public NOAA NP.xy data with no dome derivation; the new detail_verdict_text adds a labeled defender-pivot rebuttal with three counters (verifiability ≠ derivation; W019 self-contradiction; structural impossibility from axial-symmetric H(r)); the new detail_extra carries the W019 5.5× vs WIN-043 2.26× contrast, an Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-ref, and a forward-falsification flag for PRED-R004 (≥2.0× through 2028) using the C3-computed 1.56× single-year and decelerating trend.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py fetches NOAA NP.xy and reports total drift speed via empirical exponential fit; it does NOT decompose velocity into longitudinal/latitudinal components, never computes their ratio, and does not reference 2.26 anywhere in source. The 2.26× is a homepage/text assertion only — confirmed in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-043.json which independently audited the dome repo.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No code computes 2.26×. monitor.py has none entry for this WIN — confirmed in code_analysis.monitoring=none and corroborated by the c3 review.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None for this specific ratio — but the dome silently equivocates between W019 (weekly window, observed 5.5× lateral) and WIN-043 (unspecified multi-decade window, claimed 2.26×). No methodology spec ties either to a particular epoch.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new — c3 audit holds. The integrated detail_extra correctly says monitor.py never decomposes velocity into directional components.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain entry for the longitudinal/latitudinal ratio. Verified in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-043.json:suggested_text — fetch_nmp_history extracts lat/lon and computes total drift speed only, never the directional ratio. code_analysis.monitoring=none in current data/wins.json.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard core-mantle-boundary geophysics (flux lobes, Livermore et al. 2020 Nat Geosci) explains the same directional asymmetry without invoking dome geometry. The 2.26× ratio is descriptive arithmetic over a chosen window — standard physics generates the underlying flux-lobe dynamics that produce the asymmetry.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: NOAA NP.xy spans 1590-2025 and predates the dome model by decades. The dome did not pre-register a value of 2.26× before observation; it adopted the value after the published trajectory existed. monitor.py has no entry for this ratio so even the weak commit-timestamp test is inapplicable. detail_verdict_text correctly flags this under Part 3.1b.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site or in its source files derives 2.26× from disc_radius (20015), firmament_height (8537/8619), sun_altitude (5733), or moon_altitude (2534). H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) is a function of r alone (Axial Symmetry Paradox) — by construction it cannot generate longitude-dependent quantities of any magnitude. detail_verdict_text counter (3) and detail_extra Structural Impossibility paragraph both make this point with the verified #axial-symmetry anchor.

holes_found.description: Wording inaccuracy in the new defender-pivot paragraph (detail_verdict_text counter (2)): the sentence "A model that produces two different numbers for the same physical asymmetry, both labeled as confirmation, is not pointing — it is reading off whatever the data says." overstates. WIN-043 is labeled CONFIRMED; W019 is labeled REFINED — they are NOT both labeled as confirmation. The detail_extra paragraph correctly distinguishes the two statuses ("2.26× as a CONFIRMED past claim... 5.5× in W019's current REFINED notes"), so the imprecision is internal-inconsistent across the two new paragraphs.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "both labeled as confirmation" with "both presented as supportive of the dome's NMP narrative" or "both pointed to as confirming the asymmetry." Alternatively: "two different numbers for the same physical asymmetry, presented in CONFIRMED and REFINED statuses respectively" — keeps the contradiction force without misstating the labels.

holes_found.description: Defender pivot anticipated paragraph counter (2) does not directly preempt the time-window methodological distinction. A dome defender will say: "WIN-043's 2.26× is a multi-decade average; W019's 5.5× is a single-week observation. These ARE different methodologies measuring different things — of course they differ. There's no contradiction between a long-term ratio and a short-term ratio." This is the strongest defender pivot on the W019 rebuttal and our paragraph (2) does not directly address it.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a clause to counter (2): "Even granting that W019's 5.5× is a single-week observation while WIN-043's 2.26× is presented as a multi-decade average, the dome publishes no methodology specifying which window WIN-043 corresponds to (the same dataset yields 1.69× over 1900-2025, 2.34× over 2020-2025, and 9.43× over 2010-2025 — see detail_evidence) — so the dome cannot consistently say WHICH ratio is the prediction." This converts the W019 contrast from "contradiction" (which is overclaiming) to "unspecified-window equivocation" (which the data already supports).

holes_found.description: Citation strengthening: detail_extra forward-falsification paragraph cites "Chulliat et al. 2020" for the post-2019 NMP slowdown. Chulliat 2020 is a real reference (US/UK World Magnetic Model 2020-2025 Technical Report, NOAA/BGS) but is primarily a model-update document, not the canonical paper documenting the post-2019 slowdown. The most-cited paper for NMP acceleration→deceleration narrative is Livermore, Finlay, Bayliff (2020) Nat. Geosci. 13, 387-391 "Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation." A defender who knows the geomagnetism literature could note our citation is plausible but not the strongest source.
holes_found.recommendation: Add Livermore et al. 2020 as either a replacement or co-citation: "per the post-2019 NMP slowdown (Livermore et al. 2020; Chulliat et al. 2020)." The Livermore paper directly characterizes the slowdown mechanism (flux-lobe elongation reducing the effective driving force) and is the more commonly-cited authority for the slowdown trend.

stronger_arguments.description: Window-sensitivity table: detail_evidence already says the ratio is "epoch-dependent" but the c3 quantitative_verification computed actual values for many windows (1900-2025: 1.69×; 1990-2025: 2.16×; 2000-2025: 2.70×; 2010-2025: 9.43×; 2020-2025: 2.34×; 2024-2025: 1.56×). Surfacing 2-3 of these values inline (rather than the abstract "epoch-dependent" framing) makes the unspecified-window critique concrete. A dome defender pivot has no comeback against "the same dataset yields 1.69× to 9.43× depending on window choice" — the dome must either name the canonical window (and lose the freedom to claim other windows confirm) or admit there is no canonical window (and lose the prediction).

PRED-R004 quantitative gate: detail_extra says "if the trend continues" PRED-R004 falsifies, with the 2024-2025 single-year 1.56× as the leading data point. A stronger version would set an explicit gate: "For PRED-R004 to remain confirmed through 2028, the cumulative km/yr ratio across the test window must hold ≥2.0× — currently 2.16× (1990-2025) but trending downward. A weighted projection using the 2020-2025 → 2024-2025 deceleration would put the 1990-2028 ratio near 2.0× — within noise of falsification before 2028." This converts the soft "watchable" framing into a quantitative test.

kernel_of_truth.description: The directional asymmetry of NMP drift is real and quantitatively significant: in km/yr units, longitudinal velocity has dominated latitudinal velocity for the entire post-1990 trajectory (data/wins.json:WIN-043.detail_evidence describes the NOAA NP.xy source). Independent reconstruction this cycle (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-043.c3.json:quantitative_verification.computations) recovers ~2.16× over 1990-2025, within 5% of the dome's claimed 2.26× — confirming the dome did not fabricate the number, even if it adopted it post-hoc. The dome correctly identified a feature (NMP directional asymmetry) that most lay readers would not extract from the same dataset without prompting; that pointing-at work has nontrivial pedagogical value (data/uncounted-failures.json:FAIL-003 documents that the dome registered W019 specifically to track this asymmetry, even though W019 ultimately failed in direction).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three independent failure layers persist after granting the kernel, and each is now properly surfaced in the integrated text. (1) Post-hoc retrodiction: NOAA NP.xy predates the dome model by decades (data/wins.json:WIN-043.detail_evidence), and the dome has no monitor.py entry that timestamps a value before measurement (monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-043.json confirms zero computational infrastructure for the ratio). (2) Self-contradiction (with the time-window caveat from hole #2 above): the dome's own W019 (data/uncounted-failures.json:FAIL-003) reports 5.5× lateral:poleward in REFINED notes, vs WIN-043's 2.26× CONFIRMED, with no published methodology distinguishing which window WIN-043 corresponds to. (3) Structural impossibility: the dome's H(r) is axially symmetric (raw-text/05-model.txt; cross-ref docs/index.html#axial-symmetry verified at line 1146 of the rendered walkthrough) and cannot generate longitude-dependent quantities of any magnitude. Any one of these would be sufficient to disqualify WIN-043 as a derivation rather than a description; together they form the cumulative case detail_verdict_text and detail_extra now make.

deeper_analysis: EXP-297 integration substantively closed the c3/c4 holes (W019 self-contradiction surfaced; Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-referenced via verified #axial-symmetry anchor; PRED-R004 forward falsification flagged). The integration matches the EXP-297 patch specs verbatim — find/replace P1 produced the Defender-pivot anticipated paragraph at the end of detail_verdict_text; field_set P2 populated detail_extra from null to a 1574-char block. ISS-1918, ISS-1902, ISS-1903 are correctly closed. Three residual issues remain: (a) MINOR wording inaccuracy in detail_verdict_text counter (2) — "both labeled as confirmation" misstates the dome's status taxonomy (WIN-043 is CONFIRMED, W019 is REFINED); the detail_extra paragraph on the same point gets it right, so this is internal-inconsistent. (b) MODERATE missing time-window preempt — the strongest defender pivot on the W019 contrast ("different windows = different ratios, no contradiction") is not directly preempted in counter (2). The fix is to fold the existing detail_evidence "epoch-dependent" framing into the new paragraph. (c) MINOR citation specificity — Livermore et al. 2020 (Nat. Geosci.) is the more canonical citation for the post-2019 NMP slowdown than Chulliat et al. 2020 (model-update technical report); both can co-exist. None of these undermine the integrated case; all are tightening edits. The verdict "Std Model Explains" remains correct: the dome has no derivation, standard geophysics handles the underlying mechanism, and the structural-impossibility argument from H(r) closes the geometric escape route. A verdict change to "Self-Contradicted" was considered and rejected: the W019/WIN-043 numerical mismatch is real but admits the time-window defense (hole #2), which makes "contradiction" stronger language than the evidence directly supports without the additional preempt. Continue as "Std Model Explains" with a stronger detail_extra carrying the cumulative case.

citations_verified: NOAA NGDC NP.xy (https://ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/data/poles/NP.xy) — 437 lines, 1590-2025, fetched and computed in c3 (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-043.c3.json:quantitative_verification.computations)
docs/index.html#axial-symmetry — verified to exist at line 1146 of current build
data/predictions.json:PRED-R004 — confirmed claim is "NMP drift ratio ≥2.0× through 2028" with test_window_closes 2028-12-31, our_verdict=recycled
data/uncounted-failures.json:FAIL-003 — confirms W019 dome_label=refined, outcome=lateral 5.5× larger than poleward, dome_ref_namespace=weekly, dome_ref_current=W019

monitor/changes/chg-20260420-0414-001.json — confirms W019 currently in dome's REFINED set ("16 REFINED occurrences (same as legacy W019/W020/W027/PRED-R006 set)") and predictions page hash unchanged for 39+ polls so the REFINED status persists
Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out — anchor #posthoc-standard exists in detail_verdict_text reference
citations_unchecked: Chulliat et al. 2020 — citation is plausible (Chulliat is a real NOAA geomagnetism researcher with 2020 publications including the US/UK WMM 2020-2025 Technical Report) but does not directly document the post-2019 slowdown narrative; Livermore et al. 2020 Nat. Geosci. 13, 387-391 is the more canonical citation. See hole #3.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Speaking as the dome defender post-EXP-297: your new defender-pivot paragraph attacks a strawman. Your counter (2) (W019 contradiction) compares a multi-decade average (WIN-043: 2.26×) to a single-week measurement (W019: 5.5×) and calls that a contradiction. It isn't — those are different time windows measuring different things. The "two different numbers for the same asymmetry" framing only works if both are measuring the same window, which they aren't. Your counter (3) (axial symmetry from H(r)) is technically correct but misses our claim: we are not claiming the asymmetry comes from H(r) alone — we claim aetheric vortex dynamics (which can be longitude-dependent in our richer model) produce the asymmetry, and H(r) is the *altitude* component, not the full geometric description. So your structural impossibility argument applies only to a strawman version of our model.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Hole #2 above (the time-window preempt) directly closes the first half of this defense. For the second half ("H(r) is altitude only, aetheric vortex is the longitude-dependent part"), our review already addresses this elsewhere: the Axial Symmetry Paradox section catalogs that the dome has no published derivation of any longitude-dependent aetheric quantity — "aetheric vortex" is a label not a formula. If we want to fully close this defense in WIN-043 specifically, add a parenthetical to counter (3): "or any other dome-published equation — see Section 4 Axial Symmetry Paradox for the catalog of dome equations and the longitude-independence proof." That bumps defense_survives from 2 to 1. Defense already weakened from c3's 3 to current 2 by EXP-297's integration.

cross_win_consistency.consistency_notes: WIN-043 is part of the WIN-040..043 + 059-061 + 063 detail_group. All draw from the same NOAA NP.xy dataset. The new detail_extra correctly cross-references the Axial Symmetry Paradox section which catalogs all of these as longitude-dependent claims from a radially-symmetric model. No cross-WIN inconsistency introduced by EXP-297; the new content reinforces the systemic structural argument. WIN-007 (which the existing detail_evidence already references) shows the same dataset is used for the pole position and acceleration claims — consistent attribution.

claims_checked: EXP-297 P1: detail_verdict_text post-EXP length should ~= pre-length 1136 + new paragraph length
EXP-297 P2: detail_extra should be non-null after integration
5.5× lateral:poleward attribution to W019 REFINED notes
quantitative_verification.computations: detail_verdict_text length is 3152 (was 1136 in c3), Δ=2016 chars — matches the EXP-297 P1 replace string (~2050 chars including HTML). detail_extra length is 1574 (was 0/null in c3), populated as expected. PRED-R004 confirmed in data/predictions.json:entries to claim "NMP drift ratio ≥2.0× through 2028" with test_window_closes 2028-12-31. 5.5× attribution to W019 cross-checked against data/uncounted-failures.json:FAIL-003 (outcome: "lateral motion 5.5× larger than poleward") and monitor/changes/chg-20260420-0414-001.json (W019 in dome REFINED set; predictions page byte-stable for 39+ polls). #axial-symmetry anchor verified at docs/index.html line 1146 (id="axial-symmetry").
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None — all numeric claims in the new content are grounded. The ratio numbers (2.26× claimed, 2.16× recovered, 5.5× W019, 1.56× single-year) are consistent with c3 quantitative_verification.computations and with FAIL-003. The 2.4× contrast (5.5/2.26 ≈ 2.434) checks. The PRED-R004 ≥2.0× threshold matches data/predictions.json verbatim.

summary_for_decider: EXP-297 integration successful — c3 holes #1 (W019 self-contradiction) and #2 (Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-ref) are properly closed in the integrated text. ISS-1918/1902/1903 closure is justified. Three residual TIGHTENING edits (1 moderate, 2 minor) recommended but none undermine the case. Verdict "Std Model Explains" holds. Recommended action: minor_edit. Suggest the next decider patch fold (a) the time-window preempt into the defender-pivot paragraph counter (2), (b) replace "both labeled as confirmation" wording, and (c) add Livermore et al. 2020 to the Chulliat citation. Total prose delta ~150-300 chars. After those edits, defense_survives drops from 2 to 1.
